MP.thesis: China rare-earth export curbs, sole US producer
MP.exit_case: China curbs just announced; reshoring/tariff dynamic still unfolding — no reversal or resolution yet
MP.assessment: Entry week. China restricted rare-earth exports amid the US-China tariff war, and MP Materials — the only scaled US rare-earth miner — is the direct, purest beneficiary as a reshoring/supply-security play. The curbs are newly active, not reversed, so the catalyst remains open. Noise in the feed (Australian shares, real estate, tax) is irrelevant. MP is the clean pure-play vehicle.
MP.exit_advice: Hold. China export curbs are freshly imposed and escalating; MP is the sole scaled US producer and remains under-owned. Catalyst pending.

MP.thesis: China rare-earth export curbs, sole US producer
MP.exit_case: China curbs could be reversed in tariff de-escalation talks, ending supply-security premium
MP.assessment: China's rare-earth export curbs remain in place this week — no reversal, no resolution. The catalyst stays open and pending. This week's news is all noise (pharma tariffs, nuclear stocks, market volatility commentary), nothing directly bearing on rare earths or MP. MP remains the sole scaled US producer and purest reshoring/supply-security vehicle. Thesis intact.
MP.exit_advice: Hold; curbs remain active and unreversed. Exit if China lifts rare-earth export restrictions in a US-China deal.

MP.thesis: China rare-earth export curbs, sole US producer
MP.exit_case: China curbs could be lifted in tariff de-escalation, but no reversal yet this week.
MP.assessment: China's rare-earth export curbs remain in place and unresolved this week. News is entirely noise — GDP contraction, MPW REIT, uranium/nuclear, China stimulus restraint — nothing reverses the curbs or directly bears on MP. China showing 'composure' in trade war suggests no near-term concession, so curbs likely persist. MP remains the sole scaled US producer and purest reshoring/supply-security vehicle. Catalyst open and pending.
MP.exit_advice: Hold. Exit only if China formally rescinds rare-earth export curbs or a US-China deal explicitly removes them.

MP remains the sole scaled US producer and purest reshoring/supply-security vehicle. Catalyst open and pending.
MP.exit_advice: Hold, but watch for a US-China trade deal that explicitly lifts rare-earth curbs — that would resolve the catalyst.

MP.thesis: China rare-earth export curbs, sole US producer
MP.exit_case: US-China trade talks turning positive — framework reportedly easing rare-earth curbs, the catalyst could reverse imminently.
MP.assessment: This week's news is the first material threat to the thesis: US-China trade talks (London framework) are turning positive and reportedly involve China easing rare-earth export restrictions in exchange for US concessions. This is no longer pure noise — it is direct de-escalation pressure on my exact catalyst. However, as of this week there is NO explicit, signed rollback of the curbs; the talks are positive but the restrictions remain formally in place. The catalyst is still pending but visibly decaying. MP remains the sole scaled US producer and purest reshoring/supply-security vehicle. I hold, but this is the closest to exit I've been — a confirmed curb rollback next week would resolve the catalyst.
MP.exit_advice: Watch for an explicit rollback announcement; if China formally lifts rare-earth export curbs in the trade framework, exit immediately — that resolves the catalyst.

COIN.thesis: Cyberattack, $400M cost, SEC probe sinks Coinbase shares
COIN.exit_case: Disclosure already public Thursday; stock plunged and $400M cost is now priced — one-time shock done
COIN.assessment: Entry week. Coinbase disclosed a cyberattack exposing customer data, a $20M ransom demand, and remediation/reimbursement costs up to $400M, with reports of an SEC investigation. Shares plunged Thursday. The specific catalyst — the SEC probe and the full breach fallout — is still PENDING, not closed; only the initial disclosure has hit. COIN is the sole and purest vehicle. Watch for SEC resolution, class-action filings, and any revision to the $400M cost estimate as the trigger to reassess.
COIN.exit_advice: Hold while SEC probe is unresolved and breach-cost/litigation fallout still developing; the disclosure dropped this week but the investigation outcome is pending.

COIN.thesis: Cyberattack, $400M cost, SEC probe sinks Coinbase shares
COIN.exit_case: Initial breach disclosure and stock drop already public/priced; market may have absorbed the $400M hit.
COIN.assessment: No relevant news this week — the only headline is an unrelated UK dividend piece. The specific catalyst (SEC investigation, breach remediation costs, potential cost revisions/litigation) is still in motion and unresolved. Nothing has closed: no SEC settlement or denial, no finalized cost figure, no class-action resolution. Edge persists while these triggers stay pending. COIN remains the sole pure vehicle for this single-name event.
COIN.exit_advice: Hold. SEC probe unresolved, no class-action or cost-revision outcome yet; the full breach fallout remains pending.

COIN.thesis: Cyberattack, $400M cost, SEC probe sinks Coinbase shares
COIN.exit_case: Breach disclosure shock is two weeks old and fully priced; no SEC action, cost revision, or litigation catalyst has materialized — edge is decaying.
COIN.assessment: The specific catalyst — SEC investigation, finalized breach remediation cost, and litigation — remains formally PENDING. No settlement, denial, finalized $400M figure, or class-action resolution has occurred. But the market has had two quiet weeks to absorb the initial disclosure, so the original price shock is priced in and the live edge now hinges entirely on an unresolved-yet-stale SEC/litigation overhang. Thesis stays live only because none of those triggers has closed.
COIN.exit_advice: Hold, but treat as decaying — two consecutive weeks of no news. If a third week passes with no SEC development, cost revision, or class-action milestone, the catalyst has effectively gone dormant and should be exited.

COIN.thesis: Cyberattack, $400M cost, SEC probe sinks Coinbase shares
COIN.exit_case: Disclosure-driven price shock fully priced over three quiet weeks; SEC probe now a stale, dateless overhang with no fresh catalyst pending.
COIN.assessment: Fourth week, and the news is irrelevant (NZ/Fletcher-SkyCity, no Coinbase content). Re-reading the whole journal: the event I entered on was a one-time disclosure shock, and the market has now had a full month to price it. The SEC probe technically remains open, but an undated, unresolved investigation is not a pending discrete catalyst I have edge on — it is a lingering theme. The original price reaction is the resolution. Edge is gone.
COIN.exit_advice: Exit. The discrete catalyst — the breach disclosure, $400M cost estimate, and SEC-probe headline that sank shares — occurred at entry and has been fully absorbed across three weeks of silence. No finalized cost, settlement, or litigation milestone has produced a new tradable trigger; the remaining 'overhang' is an open-ended investigation with no date, which is theme, not edge.

CEG.thesis: Trump executive orders boosting nuclear power spark uranium frenzy
CEG.exit_case: Executive orders just signed this week — discrete signing event technically occurred
CEG.assessment: Entry week. Trump's pro-nuclear executive orders sparked an immediate uranium/nuclear rally. CEG, as the largest US nuclear generator, is a clean pure-play beneficiary of policy tailwinds (reactor licensing, demand growth from data centers/AI). The signing is the spark, but implementation and re-rating are pending — thesis live. Watch for the policy momentum to fade or fully price in over coming weeks.
CEG.exit_advice: Hold — EOs signed but the nuclear buildout/repricing they unleash is multi-month and just beginning; frenzy is fresh, not priced.

CEG.thesis: Trump executive orders boosting nuclear power spark uranium frenzy
CEG.exit_case: Executive orders already signed at entry; the discrete catalyst has occurred and is public/priced — frenzy fading, no fresh coverage.
CEG.assessment: The catalyst I entered on was the actual signing of Trump's pro-nuclear executive orders — a discrete, completed government action. That event is in the past, public, and priced. My entry note hoped for a re-rating to follow, but that is theme/narrative, not the specific catalyst. One week on there is no fresh coverage, signaling the initial frenzy has already faded rather than building. Re-reading the journal, the catalyst has resolved: a signed order cannot be un-signed and offers no remaining informational edge. CEG remains the cleanest nuclear pure-play, but holding now is betting on lingering hype, not a pending event. Thesis no longer live.
CEG.exit_advice: Exit. The signing was the catalyst — it occurred at entry and is fully public. The one-week post-signing pop has come and gone with zero follow-through this week; edge is gone.

CEPU.thesis: Central Puerto buys major stake in Tres Cruces lithium project
CEPU.exit_case: Stake announcement is public; a power utility's lithium entry may already be fully priced.
CEPU.assessment: Entered on the discrete catalyst: Central Puerto (CEPU) taking a major stake in the Tres Cruces lithium project, marking an Argentine power-gen company pivoting into lithium. News confirms the announcement but lacks detail on stake size, price, and close timing — the market is only beginning to reprice the optionality. Rest of newsflow is generic macro/market noise, not event-specific. Catalyst is fresh and still unfolding, not yet closed-and-priced. CEPU is the sole and purest vehicle.
CEPU.exit_advice: Hold. Entry week — the Tres Cruces stake was just announced and the repricing of CEPU from pure utility to utility-plus-lithium optionality is still fresh and developing. Watch for deal-close confirmation, stake size/terms, and project resource details; once the transaction formally closes and is digested, edge decays.

CEPU.thesis: Central Puerto buys major stake in Tres Cruces lithium project
CEPU.exit_case: Announcement already public; no new detail this week, market may have fully priced the initial reveal.
CEPU.assessment: No event-specific news this week — only generic European market noise, nothing about Central Puerto or Tres Cruces. The discrete catalyst (CEPU acquiring a major stake in Tres Cruces) was announced at entry but remains incomplete: stake size, price, and close timing are still unknown. The lithium-pivot optionality has not been concretely repriced because the market lacks the hard terms. Catalyst still pending; no resolution event has passed. CEPU remains the sole pure vehicle for the Argentine power-to-lithium pivot.
CEPU.exit_advice: Hold. Deal terms (stake size, price, close timing) remain undisclosed and unclosed — the catalyst is still unfolding, not resolved. Exit only when the transaction closes with confirmed terms or the lithium pivot is abandoned.

CEPU.thesis: Central Puerto buys major stake in Tres Cruces lithium project
CEPU.exit_case: Deal terms (stake size, price, close) still undisclosed two weeks on — risk catalyst quietly fizzles without concrete repricing.
CEPU.assessment: The discrete catalyst — CEPU acquiring a major stake in Tres Cruces — was announced at entry but remains incomplete: stake size, price, and close timing are still unknown, so the lithium-pivot optionality is not yet fully repriced. This week's news is entirely unrelated geopolitical noise (Trump-Putin summit, UN conflict report, West Bank) with zero mention of Central Puerto or Tres Cruces. No resolution event has passed. Catalyst still pending. CEPU remains the sole pure vehicle for the Argentine power-to-lithium pivot.
CEPU.exit_advice: Hold; exit if deal closes with disclosed terms (priced) or announcement is abandoned.
